channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from trusted sources worldwide

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany unveils first post-WWII military strategy to become Europe's strongest army by 2039
item.description: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius presented a new national defense strategy marking Germany's first such plan since World War II, as Russia's growing threat drives European rearmament. The Bundeswehr will move from rigid hardware quotas to a flexible, effects-based planning model.

item.title: 🇪🇸 Spanish Defense Minister announces 100 armored vehicles for Ukraine during Kyiv visit
item.description: Spain will send ammunition and 100 armored vehicles to Ukraine, Defense Minister Margarita Robles announced during a visit to Kyiv, calling it a testament to Spain's commitment to democratic values and a just peace.

item.title: ⚓ Navy Secretary John Phelan removed from post amid Iran war and Pentagon clashes
item.description: Trump's top Navy official John Phelan was abruptly removed from his position immediately, the Pentagon confirmed without providing a reason. The departure comes as the US Navy enforces a high-stakes blockade of Iran and follows the firing of a top US Army general.

item.title: 🇳🇱 Russia could be ready for NATO conflict within a year after Ukraine, Dutch intelligence warns
item.description: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat the alliance militarily but to divide it through limited territorial gains, according to a new Dutch intelligence report as European nations accelerate defense preparations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 CIA agents deaths in Mexico spark sovereignty row between Mexico and White House
item.description: The deaths of CIA agents in Mexico have triggered a diplomatic dispute with the White House, raising questions about US intelligence operations on Mexican soil.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇶💰 US blocks Iraq dollar shipments to pressure Tehran
item.description: The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq, forcing Baghdad to distance itself from Iran. The money comes from Iraq own oil revenues but the US controls its delivery.

item.title: 🇮🇳🗳️ Millions of voters purged from rolls before West Bengal election
item.description: India has removed millions from electoral rolls ahead of critical state elections. Critics say Muslims and minorities have been disproportionately affected by the voter list cleanup.

item.title: 💰⚔️ Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
item.description: The world largest condom producer Karex warns prices may rise sharply as rubber supplies are disrupted by conflict in the Middle East. The Malaysia-based company supplies global markets.

item.title: ⚖️🌱 Federal judge strikes down Trump clean energy slowdown
item.description: A federal judge has blocked Trump administration actions that slowed clean energy projects, ruling the moves exceeded executive authority. The decision affects wind and solar developments.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 Anti-Trump sentiment unites European politics
item.description: Trump brand has sunk so low that conservatives are joining leftist politicians in opposing him, creating unexpected political unity across Europe. The dynamic is reshaping electoral coalitions.

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as Iran war doubles jet fuel prices
item.description: German airline Lufthansa will cancel 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer, citing soaring fuel costs from the Strait of Hormuz closure. The carrier will stop flying to 10 cities and retire 27 aircraft as jet fuel prices have doubled since the US-Iran conflict began.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇰🇷 US restricts intelligence sharing with South Korea after minister reveals North Korean nuclear site
item.description: Washington has partially restricted satellite intelligence sharing with Seoul after South Korea's unification minister publicly identified a suspected North Korean uranium enrichment facility in Kusong. The move highlights growing tensions in the US-South Korea alliance under President Lee Jae Myung's conciliatory approach to Pyongyang.

item.title: ⛪🇬🇶 Pope Leo condemns prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, calls for freedom
item.description: During his Africa tour finale, Pope Leo criticized Equatorial Guinea's prison conditions and urged the oil-rich nation to use its resources for all citizens, not just the elite. He addressed 100,000 people including President Obiang, the world's longest-serving leader, ahead of a visit to an infamous prison.

item.title: ⚖️🇸🇻 El Salvador begins mass trial of 486 alleged MS-13 gang members
item.description: A Salvadoran court started one of the biggest mass trials under President Bukele's emergency powers, prosecuting 486 alleged MS-13 members for over 47,000 crimes. Human rights groups warn the collective prosecution violates due process and blocks access to legal counsel.

item.title: 🇨🇴🇦🇷 Argentina arrests suspect in assassination of Colombian presidential hopeful Miguel Uribe
item.description: Argentine authorities arrested a Colombian man suspected of logistical involvement in the assassination of Senator Miguel Uribe, who was shot during a campaign event in June 2025 and died in August. Prosecutors believe the attack was orchestrated by a FARC dissident group called Second Marquetalia.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Second French peacekeeper dies after Lebanon ambush blamed on Hezbollah
item.description: Anicet Girardin became the third French soldier to die since the start of Middle East fighting, following an ambush that highlights the dangers facing UN peacekeeping forces in southern Lebanon.

item.title: 🇹🇼 US condemns China after African states block Taiwan president eSwatini flight
item.description: A State Department official called the incident an abuse of the international civil aviation system, after African countries prevented Taiwan leader from transiting to eSwatini.

item.title: ⚽ Trump envoy pushes to replace Iran with Italy in World Cup
item.description: The plan is an attempt to mend ties with Italian PM Meloni after she and Trump fell out over his attacks against Pope Leo, according to a report.

item.title: 🚢 Iran ramps up ship attacks in Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire extension
item.description: With Iran refusing to commit to peace talks, the future of the ceasefire remains in doubt as Iranian forces continue targeting vessels in the strategic waterway.

item.title: ⚔️ US Navy destroyer fires deck gun at ship for first time since 1988
item.description: USS Spruance engaged another vessel with its deck gun in a first for the Navy in nearly four decades, reflecting the escalation in maritime conflict in the region.

item.title: 🚢 Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz as blockade standoff escalates
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard fired on three commercial vessels and seized two of them — MSC Francesca and Epaminondas — in a major escalation of the Hormuz maritime conflict. The White House said the seizures do not violate the US-Iran ceasefire because the ships were not American or Israeli.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran says reopening Strait of Hormuz 'impossible' amid US blockade
item.description: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf declared it 'not possible' to reopen the strategic waterway while the US maintains its naval blockade of Iranian ports, calling the blockade a 'blatant violation' of the ceasefire. President Pezeshkian said the blockade and threats are obstacles to genuine negotiations.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaves Trump administration immediately
item.description: Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan's departure 'effective immediately' with Undersecretary Hung Cao named acting secretary. Phelan is the latest high-ranking military leader to exit as the US enforces a naval blockade against Iran.

item.title: 📰 Israeli strike kills journalist in Lebanon after blocking rescue efforts
item.description: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon after rescuers were blocked from accessing the site by further Israeli fire. Lebanon's prime minister called the targeting of journalists and obstruction of relief 'war crimes'.

item.title: 🗳️ High-stakes West Bengal election begins amid voter roll controversy
item.description: Voters in India's West Bengal state head to the polls in a critical contest where about 9 million voters — 12% of the electorate — were removed from electoral rolls. PM Modi's BJP is mounting an aggressive push to unseat Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress.
